creator: william88@gmail.com
text:
On Tue, Mar 5, 2013 at 3:12 PM, Ruslan Makhmatkhanov <cvs-src@yandex.ru>wrote:

> Hi William,
>
> could you please list the ports it breaks? 2.0 - is indeed python3 only,
> but 2.1 (that is in ports) is both for python2 and python3 (via py-six).
> All the "breakages" were not API breakages but rather artificial
> limitations of supported dateutil version in package's setup.py and/or
> port's Makefile.
>

Well, there are several packages that explictly requires dateutil>=1.5 and
< 2.0 via setup.py. py-sanetime, py-rhodecode and py-celery are the ones
that come to mind right now, but I'm sure there are more.
I figured it would be a lot easier to rebocopy dateutil than patch dozens
of setup.py files in ports.



-- 
William Grzybowski
